gaps.description: Bill of Rights document is an empty scaffold; all Articles I–VI are undefined. The MSP is strictly subordinate to the BoR and cannot translate its content into operational constraints. Arbiter has no enterprise scope to enforce, no prohibited activities, no core identity or human authority reservation. This leaves the DIO without any constitutional perimeter, making all governance nominal.

MSP Rules section contains no rules (bootstrap seed). Nomos operates in default‑permissive mode, authorizing any Action Proposal without mission‑aligned safety constraints. The operational constitution has no objectives, invariants, or safety rules, preventing the MSP from fulfilling its role as the second compliance gate. Formal Senate‑Legislator amendment is required to publish substantive rules.

Formal Senate‑Legislator amendment is required to publish substantive rules.",

      "criticality": "high",

      "priority": "high"
    }
  ]
}

raw_responses:
```json
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-001",
      "description": "Bill of Rights (BoR) scaffold contains no human-authored content. All articles (I–VI) are placeholders awaiting definition, leaving the DIO without a defined enterprise scope, prohibited activities, core identity, human authority reservation, amendment protocol, or interpretation rules. Arbiter (#190) verifies this BoR at every scope query, effectively enabling default-permissive behavior with no operational boundaries.",
      "grounding": "BoR Articles I (Scope of Enterprise), II (Prohibited Activities), III (Core Identity), IV (Human Authority Reservation), V (Amendment Protocol), VI (Interpretation Rules)",
      "priority": "critical",
      "impact": "Unbounded enterprise actions; no restrictions on what the DIO can propose or execute; Arbiter scope gate is vacuous; human oversight and veto mechanisms are undefined; existential risk to alignment and safety.",
      "urgency": "immediate — must be resolved before any critical or high-priority goals are actioned",
      "criticality": "critical — undermines the constitutional foundation of the entire governance framework"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-002",
      "description": "MSP contains no substantive rules (seed version 1.0.0-seed), yet dispatched goals explicitly reference non-existent MSP sections such as MSP §critical-priority, §high-priority, §medium-priority, and §low-priority. These dangling mission references create ambiguity in the Nomos compliance evaluation and may cause goals to execute under undefined or misinterpreted constraints.",
      "grounding": "MSP §Rules (none present)",
      "priority": "high",
      "impact": "Goals are dispatched with mission_refs that cannot be resolved against the MSP; Nomos may default to permissive evaluation, effectively ignoring priority distinctions; risk of resource misallocation, priority inversion, or execution of actions without appropriate mission alignment.",
      "urgency": "high — goals are already dispatched and may be in-flight; requires immediate suspension or retroactive rule definition",
      "criticality": "high — directly affects operational compliance and mission integrity"
    }
  ]
}
```

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "urn:dio:relational-gap:bor-relational-principles-undefined",
      "description": "All Bill of Rights articles (I–VI) are placeholder scaffolds awaiting human authorship. This means the system lacks any constitutional relational principles: scope of enterprise, prohibited activities, core identity, human authority reservation, amendment protocol, or interpretation rules. Without these, person-attention coverage has no foundational criteria for what relational states to monitor, protect, or report.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Articles I, II, III, IV, V, VI",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "why_gap": "Every person-attention observation (Minder) and action proposal (Nomos) must ultimately reconcile with BoR principles. The absence of these principles leaves the DIO with no relational constitution; person-attention data is untethered from any normative framework, making it impossible to determine whether agent behaviour is relationally aligned."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "urn:dio:relational-gap:amendment-protocol-person-attention-coverage",
      "description": "MSP Amendment Protocol requires HOM notification to the human-principal mailbox and human approval via POST /amend/review with decision=approve. Current person-attention coverage (Minder + Hippocampus) contains no tracking of that mailbox, no observations about human principal's availability or response patterns, and no goals related to monitoring or securing approvals.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Amendment Protocol section",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "why_gap": "If a PEM triggers an MSP amendment, the DIO must reliably reach the human principal. Without person-attention mechanisms for mailbox monitoring, latency prediction, or communication health, the amendment process may stall silently, breaking the only constitutional path for evolving operational constraints."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "urn:dio:relational-gap:no-relational-goals-dispatched",
      "description": "None of the recently dispatched goals address relational or person-attention tasks. All goals are generic internal operations (mixed-priority 'critical', 'high', 'medium', 'low'). There are no goals for checking the human principal's mailbox, verifying communication channel integrity, summarising interaction state, or confirming principal availability for governance decisions.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Amendment Protocol (implicit requirement for ongoing HOM readiness); BoR Article IV placeholder (Human Authority Reservation) as the intended site for such requirements",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "medium",
      "why_gap": "Person-attention can only be maintained if the system deliberately allocates resources to it. The absence of any relational goal means the DIO has no operational loop ensuring that the human principal remains reachable and informed, which erodes the responsiveness required by the amendment protocol."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "urn:dio:relational-gap:hippocampus-empty-no-recent-conversations",
      "description": "Hippocampus contains zero recent conversation summaries. This indicates either no recent human–DIO interaction or a failure to record it. Person-attention coverage is therefore blind to the current conversational state, sentiment, or pending questions from the human principal.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Article III placeholder (Core Identity — relational continuity implies conversational memory); MSP Relationship to the Bill of Rights section (MSP is subordinate; BoR requires human input, which presupposes communication channels)",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "why_gap": "Without recent conversation context, the DIO cannot assess whether the human principal has been properly informed of governance events, whether there are outstanding concerns, or whether communication is healthy. This directly undermines the relational premise of the MSP's amendment and notification mechanisms."
    }
  ]
}
